keyEvents.impact: Young nation-state with deep regional differences never fully reconciled
Fascist dictatorship; lasting constitutional safeguards against strongman rule
Monarchy abolished; fragmented parliamentary system with frequent government turnover

keyEvents.event: Treaty of Rome founds the EEC
keyEvents.impact: European integration becomes the anchor of Italian foreign policy and economic strategy

keyEvents.event: Years of Lead and the Moro assassination
keyEvents.impact: Domestic terrorism trauma; aversion to political extremism and instability
Collapse of the postwar party system; chronic distrust of the political class
Near-default; public debt constrains every strategic ambition since

history.narrative: A founding member of NATO and the EU that punches below its economic weight, constrained by massive public debt and unstable coalition governments. Its strategic horizon is the 'enlarged Mediterranean': energy routes from North Africa, migration flows, and rivalry with Turkey for influence in Libya, all managed through multilateral frameworks rather than unilateral action.

redLines.description: Armed attack on a NATO or EU member state
Closure of Mediterranean shipping lanes or cutoff of North African energy supplies
Hostile power establishing military dominance over Libya or the central Mediterranean

goals.description: Reduce public debt and revive chronically stagnant growth
Manage Mediterranean migration flows and stabilize Libya
Secure diversified energy supplies as a Mediterranean gas hub
Preserve standing as a G7 power and core EU founding member
